title: Stepping-stone vs getting-gilded: the different use of escalator region among international students
abstract: This study explores Polish and Chinese international students in London through the escalator region framework, analyzing sixty qualitative interviews to identify distinct mobility strategies. It is found that most Polish participants employ London as a stepping-stone toward international careers, benefiting from EU mobility policies and institutional prestige, whereas most Chinese participants adopt a getting-gilded approach, attending highly ranked universities primarily to enhance their domestic employment prospects upon returning home, though significant individual variations exist within both groups. The research underscores how financial support systems differ significantly, with Chinese students relying more on family funding while Polish students demonstrate greater financial autonomy. Through a three-phase escalator model, the study demonstrates how structural factors, including university rankings, combine with individual agency and national contexts to influence mobility patterns. These findings complicate homogeneous explanations of international student mobility by revealing how particular cultural and institutional dynamics generate divergent trajectories within the same academic environment.
journal: British Journal of Sociology of Education
